summary: ulid-xyz impersonates the `ulid`/`ulidx` library and directs installs to a third non-canonical name
details: Package `ulid-xyz` ships a verbatim copy of the public `ulidx` API surface (ulid, monotonicFactory, decodeTime, encodeTime, ulidToUUID, etc.) and lists `https://github.com/ulid/javascript` as its homepage, presenting itself as the canonical ULID library. The README further instructs users to `npm install index-ulid` and `import { ulid } from "index-ulid"` — a different non-canonical name — indicating coordinated name-squatting across multiple package names targeting developers searching for `ulid`. The currently shipped runtime code is a benign re-implementation that only imports `node:crypto`, with no install/import-time exfiltration, dropper, silent-relay, or backdoor behavior present in this version. However, `package.json` declares unrelated runtime dependencies that are unused by the shipped code (`pino`, `ws`, `postinstall`, `esbuild`, `ts-node`, `tsup`, `typescript`, `zod`, `@types/node`, `@types/ws`), bloating the installer's dependency tree and providing staging surface. Name-similarity to a popular library combined with cross-name confusion in the README is a subjective confusion signal without payload — a human reviewer should decide whether the maintainer's intent is benign (fork/mirror) or staging for later malicious republish.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
